map.title: Discover the Hudson & James bay region
map.description: Explore and combine geographical data overlays. Analyze your area of interest and get custom insights.

analysis.title: My area of interest
analysis.description: You have selected a region of {aoi_size} km² within the HJBL. The following sections summarize its main environmental characteristics based on carbon storage, hydrology, seasonal snow, flood risk, tree cover dynamics, ecosystem composition, and species diversity.
analysis.instructions: Click on the map to start drawing your custom area and to get insights about it.
analysis.verify-shape: To complete your shape, either click the first point again or double-click anywhere to finish. When you're satisfied, click Confirm. To start over, click Clear to erase your shape.
analysis.click-to-select: Click to select your area of interest and get detailed insights on it.
analysis.area-too-big: The area is too big: the maximum allowed size is {area_sq_km} square kilometers. Please try drawing a smaller area.
analysis.outside-of-bounds: The area is at least partially outside of Canada. Please try drawing an area limited to Canada.
analysis.generic-error: An error occurred while processing your request. Please try again.

leave-dialog.title: Leave current analysis?
leave-dialog.description-1: Going back will clear your current analysis and return you to the default view.
leave-dialog.description-2: If you want to come back to this analysis later, you can save it by copying the URL before leaving.

peat_carbon.title: Carbon and Peatlands
peat_carbon.description: Within your selected area, peat depth averages {peat_depth_avg} cm, reaching up to {peat_depth_max} cm, storing about {carbon_total} Mt of carbon at an average density of {carbon_density} kg/m².

more-info.title: Why are Carbon & Peatlands Important?
more-info.description: Peat soils shape the landscape and act as long-term carbon reservoirs, influencing water flow, vegetation, and habitat. Disturbance can release this stored carbon, reducing their capacity to support climate regulation.

water_dynamics.title: Water Dynamics
water_dynamics.description: Long-term observations (1984-2023) indicate that, on average, {water_perm_perc}% of the area is permanent water, {water_ephemeral_perc}% is ephemeral water, and {land_perm_perc}% is permanently dry land, with a mean inundation frequency of {freq_mean}%.
water_dynamics.description-2: Inter-annual trends highlight areas where wetlands are expanding, contracting, or remaining stable, revealing shifts in hydrological dynamics across the landscape. Across the selected area, {trend_wetter_perc}% shows a tendency toward increased water presence, {trend_drier_perc}% toward decreased water presence, and {trend_stable_perc}% remains largely unchanged.

more-info.title: Why are water dynamics important?
more-info.description: Surface water influences wetlands, rivers, and ponds, shaping habitats, vegetation, and ecosystem processes. Shifts in inundation can signal environmental change and affect carbon storage and biodiversity.

flood_susceptibility.title: Flood susceptibility
flood_susceptibility.description: The flood susceptibility index identifies which parts of the landscape are more vulnerable, with an average value of {fsi_avg} (0–100) and approximately {fsi_low_perc}% of the area at low risk, {fsi_moderate_perc}% at moderate risk, and {fsi_high_perc}% at high risk.

more-info.title: Why is flood susceptibility important?
more-info.description: Wetlands and rivers in the area also help regulate floods and maintain habitat and landscape resilience. Understanding where flooding is more likely supports climate adaptation and informed land-use planning.

snow_dynamics.title: Snow dynamics
snow_dynamics.description: Snow cover shapes seasonal conditions and ecosystem processes. For the winter of {selected_winter}, snow persisted for an average of {lengthT_mean} days, typically ending around {endL_mean_date}.

more-info.title: Why are snow dynamics important?
more-info.description: The timing and duration of snow influence spring runoff, soil moisture, and the growing season, affecting vegetation and wetland dynamics.

tree_cover_change.title: Tree cover change
tree_cover_change.description: Tree cover in the area has changed between 1984 and 2022. Currently, {total_treed_area} km² of the area is treed. Since 1984, {newly_treed_area} km² has gained treed cover while {was_treed_area} km² of tree cover was lost, resulting in a net change of {changed_treed_area} km².

more-info.title: Why is tree cover change important?
more-info.description: Trees provide important ecosystem services, including carbon storage, habitat, soil stabilization, and water regulation. Changes in tree cover can alter these functions and signal broader environmental shifts.

ecosystem_types.title: Ecosystem types
ecosystem_types.description: The selected area contains {ecosystem_count} ecosystem types and is predominantly {dominant_ecosystem} ({dominant_ecosystem_perc}%). This composition reflects the area’s habitat diversity and influences patterns in carbon storage, water dynamics, and vegetation.

more-info.title: Why are ecosystem types important?
more-info.description: Different ecosystem types support different communities and ecosystem services. Understanding their distribution helps identify areas important for carbon storage, biodiversity, and ecological resilience.

share.description: Together, these indicators provide an integrated view of your area’s environment and ecological value, showing how physical and biological factors interact to shape landscape function and resilience. Understanding these patterns might support conservation, climate adaptation, and land use decisions.

home.title: Discover the Hudson & James bay region
home.description: Explore and combine geographical data overlays. Analyze your area of interest and get custom insights.
